model.label: Kimi K2.5
model.description: Moonshot AI • Reasoning model from the Kimi family built for complex thinking tasks.

pick.rationale: Michigan holds a razor-thin statistical edge as the #2 ranked team nationally with a +38.38 adjusted efficiency margin that narrowly exceeds Arizona's +37.56 mark. The Wolverines' #2 ranked defense provides a marginal advantage over the Wildcats' #3 unit, while both offenses rank similarly in the top 10. Both teams enter at essentially full strength—Arizona's Koa Peat has fully returned from his earlier leg injury, while Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg has managed his low ankle sprain effectively through the tournament. With identical 31-2 records and power conference championships from the Big Ten and Big 12 respectively, this matchup features two elite teams separated by less than one efficiency point. Michigan's slightly superior defensive metrics and marginally better overall efficiency provide a thin path to victory in what projects as a championship-caliber nail-biter between the nation's second and third best teams.

reasoningStep.title: Final Four: Arizona vs Michigan Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed advanced metrics, injury status, and recent form for both teams. Michigan ranks #2 nationally with +38.38 adj. EM (KenPom) and #2 defense vs Arizona at #3 with +37.56 adj. EM and #3 defense. Both teams 31-2 with stars healthy (Peat returned, Lendeborg playing through ankle sprain). Razor-thin margin favors Michigan's slightly superior defense and efficiency.
evidence: Michigan: KenPom #2, Torvik #2, Adj EM +38.38, Offense #8, Defense #2, Record 31-2
Arizona: KenPom #3, Torvik #3, Adj EM +37.56, Offense #7, Defense #3, Record 31-2
Koa Peat (Arizona): Returned from lower body injury, playing in tournament
Yaxel Lendeborg (Michigan): Low ankle sprain, but played in opening rounds and expects to continue
Efficiency margin gap: 0.82 points favoring Michigan
Both teams won power conference championships (Big Ten for Michigan, Big 12 for Arizona)

result.text: Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament - Detroit Sports Nation About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy Search Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home » Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament By: Don Drysdale Published: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm Modified: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm The Michigan Wolverines appear to have avoided a major injury scare just days before the start of the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament . According to reporter Alejandro Zúñiga , Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg revealed that he is dealing with a low ankle sprain , but he expects to play in the Wolverines’ NCAA Tournament opener. That is welcome news for a Michigan team that earned a No. 1 seed in the Midwest Region after posting an impressive 31–3 record during the regular season. A Key Piece of Michigan’s Title Push Lendeborg has been one of the most important players on Michigan’s roster this season. The 6-foot-9 forward has delivered strong production on both ends of the floor and is a major reason why the Wolverines are considered a legitimate national championship contender. During the 2025–26 season , Lendeborg averaged: 14.4 points per game 7.0 rebounds 3.3 assists 50.6% shooting from the field 34.3% from three-point range 82% from the free-throw line His all-around impact helped him earn 2025–26 Big Ten Player of the Year honors , along with All-Big Ten recognition . Why His Health Matters Michigan’s tournament hopes depend heavily on the versatility Lendeborg brings to the lineup. His ability to score inside, stretch the floor, and facilitate off
